name: encommon
documentation:
# Enasis Network Common Library > :warning: This project has not released its first major version. Common classes and functions used in various public and private projects.








## Documentation Read [project documentation](https://enasisnetwork.github.io/encommon/sphinx) built using the [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/) project. Should you venture into the sections below you will be able to use the `sphinx` recipe to build documention in the `sphinx/html` directory. ## Projects using library - [Enasis Network Remote Connect](https://github.com/enasisnetwork/enconnect) - [Enasis Network Homie Automate](https://github.com/enasisnetwork/enhomie) - [Enasis Network Chatting Robie](https://github.com/enasisnetwork/enrobie) - [Enasis Network Orchestrations](https://github.com/enasisnetwork/orchestro) ## Installing the package Installing stable from the PyPi repository ``` pip install encommon ``` Installing latest from GitHub repository ``` pip install git+https://github.com/enasisnetwork/encommon ``` ## Quick start for local development Start by cloning the repository to your local machine. ``` git clone https://github.com/enasisnetwork/encommon.git ``` Set up the Python virtual environments expected by the Makefile. ``` make -s venv-create ``` ### Execute the linters and tests The comprehensive approach is to use the `check` recipe. This will stop on any failure that is encountered. ``` make -s check ``` However you can run the linters in a non-blocking mode. ``` make -s linters-pass ``` And finally run the various tests to validate the code and produce coverage information found in the `htmlcov` folder in the root of the project. ``` make -s pytest ``` ## Version management > :warning: Ensure that no changes are pending. 1. Rebuild the environment. ``` make -s check-revenv ``` 1. Update the [version.txt](encommon/version.txt) file. 1. Push to the `main` branch. 1. Create [repository](https://github.com/enasisnetwork/encommon) release. 1. Build the Python package.
Be sure no uncommited files in tree. ``` make -s pypackage ``` 1. Upload Python package to PyPi test. ``` make -s pypi-upload-test ``` 1. Upload Python package to PyPi prod. ``` make -s pypi-upload-prod ```

functions.name: encommon.types.dicts.merge_dicts
documentation:
Recursively merge the contents of provided dictionaries.

:::{warning}
This function will update the `dict1` reference.
:::

# Example

```pycon
>>> dict1 = {'a': 'b', 'c': [1]}
>>> dict2 = {'a': 'B', 'c': [2]}
>>> merge_dicts(dict1, dict2)
{'a': 'b', 'c': [1, 2]}
>>> dict1
{'a': 'b', 'c': [1, 2]}
```

```
param dict1

Primary dictionary which is merged into.

param dict2

Secondary dictionary for primary updates.

param force

Force overwriting concrete values in the
primary dictionary with those from secondary. When
None only overwrites if destination is None.

param merge_list

Determines if merged or overwritten.

param merge_dict

Determines if merged or overwritten.

param paranoid

Perform an initial deepcopy on both of
the provided dictionaries before performing merges.

returns

Provided dictionary with the other merged in.
```

functions.name: encommon.types.dicts.sort_dict
documentation:
Sort the keys within the dictionary and return new one.

# Example

```pycon
>>> foo = {'b': 'be', 'a': 'ey'}
>>> sort_dict(foo)
{'a': 'ey', 'b': 'be'}
>>> sort_dict(foo, True)
{'b': 'be', 'a': 'ey'}
```

```
param value

Dictionary whose keys are sorted into new.

param reverse

Optionally reverse the sort direction.

returns

New dictionary with keys sorted alphabetical.
```

functions.name: encommon.types.lists.dedup_list
documentation:
Return the provided list values with duplicates removed.

:::{warning}
This function will update the `value` reference.
:::

# Example

```pycon
>>> value = [1, 2, 2, 3]
>>> dedup_list(value)
[1, 2, 3]
>>> value
[1, 2, 3]
```

```
param value

List of values processed for duplication.

param update

Indicate if to update provided reference.

returns

Provided list values with duplicates removed.
```

functions.name: encommon.types.lists.fuzzy_list
documentation:
Return the provided values that match provided patterns.

# Example

```pycon
>>> values = ['foo', 'bar', 'baz', 'bop']
>>> patterns = ['*o', 'ba*']
>>> fuzzy_list(values, patterns)
['foo', 'bar', 'baz']
```

```
param values

Value or values to enumerate for matching.

param patterns

Patterns which values can match any one.

returns

Provided values that match provided patterns.
```

functions.name: encommon.types.lists.inlist
documentation:
Return the boolean indicating whether needle in haystack.

# Example

```pycon
>>> haystack = [1, 2, 3]
>>> inlist(2, haystack)
True
```

```
param needle

Provided item that may be within haystack.

param haystack

List of items which may contain needle.

returns

Boolean indicating whether needle in haystack.
```

functions.name: encommon.types.notate.getate
documentation:
Collect the value within the dictionary using notation.

# Example

```pycon
>>> source = {'foo': {'bar': 'baz'}}
>>> getate(source, 'foo/bar')
'baz'
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> source = {'foo': ['bar', 'baz']}
>>> getate(source, 'foo/1')
'baz'
```

```
param source

Dictionary object processed in notation.

param path

Path to the value within the source object.

param default

Value to use if none is found in source.

param delim

Override default delimiter between parts.

returns

Value that was located within provided source.
```

functions.name: encommon.types.notate.setate
documentation:
Define the value within the dictionary using notation.

# Example

```pycon
>>> source = {'foo': {'bar': 'baz'}}
>>> source['foo']['bar']
'baz'
>>> setate(source, 'foo/bar', 'bop')
>>> source['foo']['bar']
'bop'
```

```
param source

Dictionary object processed in notation.

param path

Path to the value within the source object.

param value

Value which will be defined at noted point.

param delim

Override default delimiter between parts.
```

functions.name: encommon.types.notate.delate
documentation:
Delete the value within the dictionary using notation.

# Example

```pycon
>>> source = {'foo': {'bar': 'baz'}}
>>> delate(source, 'foo/bar')
>>> source
{'foo': {}}
```

```
param source

Dictionary object processed in notation.

param path

Path to the value within the source object.

param delim

Override default delimiter between parts.
```

methods.name: encommon.times.time.Time.shifz
documentation:
Return the new instance of object shifted using datetime.

```
param tzname

Name of the timezone associated to source.
This is not relevant in timezone included in source.

returns

New instance of the class using shifted time.
```

Interact with various time functions through one wrapper.

```
.. testsetup::
   >>> time = Time('1/1/2000 12:00am')
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> time = Time('1/1/2000 12:00am')
>>> time.stamp()
'2000-01-01T00:00:00.000000+0000'
>>> time.stamp('%m/%d/%Y')
'01/01/2000'
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> time.epoch
946684800.0
>>> time.time
datetime.time(0, 0)
>>> time.simple
'2000-01-01T00:00:00+0000'
>>> time.human
'01/01/2000 12:00AM UTC'
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> time.before
Time('1999-12-31T23:59:59.999999+0000')
>>> time.after
Time('2000-01-01T00:00:00.000001+0000')
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> time.shift('-1d')
Time('1999-12-31T00:00:00.000000+0000')
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> time.shifz('US/Central')
Time('1999-12-31T18:00:00.000000-0600')
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> time = Time('-1s')
>>> int(time.since)
1
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> time1 = Time('1/1/2000 12:00am')
>>> time2 = Time('1/1/2000 12:00am')
>>> time1 - time2
0.0
>>> time1 + time2
1893369600.0
```

```
param source

Time in various forms that will be parsed.

param anchor

Optional relative time; for snap notation.

param format

Optional format when source is timestamp.

param tzname

Name of the timezone associated to source.
This is not relevant in timezone included in source.
```

```
'min', float('-inf')

Uses the minimum time

'max', float('inf')

Uses the maximum time

object

Provide datetime or Time

2000-01-01T00:00:00Z

Uses strptime and dateutil

1645843626.2

Provide Unix epoch

'-1h@h+5m'

Provide snaptime syntax
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> parse_time('1/1/2000 12:00am')
datetime.datetime(2000, 1, 1, 0...
```

```pycon
>>> parse_time(0)
datetime.datetime(1970, 1, 1, 0...
```

```

returns

```

methods.name: encommon.times.duration.Duration.verbose
documentation:
Return the verbose format determined by source duration.

:returns: Verbose format determined by source duration.

Convert the provided seconds in a human friendly format.

# Example: Common Methods

```pycon
>>> durate = Duration(6048e4)
>>> durate.short
'1y 11mon 5d'
>>> durate.compact
'1y11mon5d'
>>> durate.verbose
'1 year, 11 months, 5 days'
```

# Example: Dump the Units

```pycon
>>> durate = Duration(6048e4)
>>> durate.units()
{'year': 1, 'month': 11, 'day': 5}
```

# Example: Disable Smart Seconds

```pycon
>>> durate = Duration(7201, False)
>>> durate.compact
'2h1s'
>>> durate.verbose
'2 hours, 1 second'
```

# Example: Basic Operators

```pycon
>>> durate1 = Duration(1000)
>>> durate2 = Duration(2000)
>>> durate1 == durate2
False
>>> durate1 != durate2
True
>>> durate1 > durate2
False
>>> durate1 >= durate2
False
>>> durate1 < durate2
True
>>> durate1 <= durate2
True
>>> durate1 + durate2
3000.0
>>> durate1 - durate2
-1000.0
>>> durate2 - durate1
1000.0
```

```
param seconds

Period in seconds that will be iterated.

param smart

Determines if we hide seconds after minute.

param groups

Determine the quantity of groups to show,
ensuring larger units are returned before smaller.
```

methods.name: encommon.colors.color.Color.hsl
documentation:
Return the value for the attribute from class instance.

:returns: Value for the attribute from class instance.

Covert colors to various forms using provided hex value.

# Example

```pycon
>>> color = Color('#003333')
>>> color.rgb
(0, 51, 51)
>>> color.hsl
(180, 100, 10)
>>> color.xyz
(1.7814, 2.6067, 3.5412)
>>> color.xy
(0.2247, 0.3287)
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> color1 = Color('#003333')
>>> color2 = Color('#330000')
>>> color1 - color2
Color('#32CCCD')
>>> color1 + color2
Color('#333333')
```

```
param source

Source color used when converting values.
```
